discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tape with VIX +5.7%, semis getting hit hard (SMH -3.8%, NVDA -4.4%), small caps underperforming (IWM -2.4%), energy the lone sector green on Middle East oil dynamics. Backdrop: inflation forecast hitting 6% Q2, 30Y yields at 5.1%, fed funds futures now pricing a HIKE — defensive/rate-sensitive names (XLU -2.3%, XLB -2.7%) and defense (ITA -3.2%) all weak. Mid-cap movers mostly look like sector-sympathy bleeds rather than idiosyncratic stories tonight.

notable_index_moves: SMH -3.8% on semis weakness despite MSFT +3% (rotation within tech)
XLE +2.36% only green sector, on Middle East oil supply concerns
ITA -3.2% defense ETF — explains KTOS/AVAV/DCO cluster
XBI -3.08% biotech weak — explains BEAM/CRSP/BBIO cluster
30Y at 5.1% + Fed hike repricing pressuring rate-sensitive XLU, XLRE, builders

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap equipment name selling off in sympathy with SMH -3.8% and NVDA -4.4%
discoveries.thesis: AEIS gets ~40%+ of revenue from semi cap equipment. Move is roughly 1.6x SMH which is in-line with typical beta for a semi-cap supplier. Two 8.01 filings on consecutive days are vague but neither appears to be earnings or restatement — likely investor conference / presentation materials. The selloff size matches the sector beta, not an idiosyncratic catalyst.
discoveries.what_confirms: If 8-K exhibits show only conference deck or routine update, confirms this is pure semi sympathy
discoveries.what_kills: If the 8.01 exhibits contain a guidance revision or customer concentration warning, then move is fundamentally driven and could extend
discoveries.catalyst: Semi sector sympathy + two 8.01 filings (likely benign)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8.01/9.01-only 8-Ks usually are presentations or routine disclosures; SMH -3.8% explains most of -6.1% at typical semi-cap beta
research_pointers: Read both 8-K exhibits to confirm they aren't guide-down or covenant disclosures
Compare AEIS YTD beta to SMH to validate sympathy thesis

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security
discoveries.setup: Defense name moving with ITA -3.2% sector ETF, today's 8-K is annual-meeting (5.07) housekeeping
discoveries.thesis: KTOS -5% with ITA -3.2% is in-line with KTOS's typical ~1.5x defense beta. The 5.07 filing is shareholder vote results — non-fundamental. Defense weakness today plausibly tied to Trump's Taiwan ambiguity comments and broader risk-off. No idiosyncratic catalyst; magnitude appropriate to sector beta. 5-day -10% suggests sector has been bleeding all week.
discoveries.what_confirms: AVAV (-4.4%) and other defense pure-plays moving in parallel confirms sector-driven move
discoveries.what_kills: If a defense-specific bearish headline (budget cut, contract loss) emerges, move could extend independent of sector
discoveries.catalyst: Defense sector sympathy (ITA -3.2%); 8-K is shareholder vote (non-material)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 5.07 = annual meeting results, definitionally non-fundamental; AVAV moving same direction with no news confirms sector driver
research_pointers: Check whether Trump Taiwan comments triggered defense procurement repricing
Watch for budget headlines into next week

discoveries.name: Ducommun Incorporated
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings drift on small-cap aerospace supplier compounded by defense sector weakness
discoveries.thesis: DCO reported earnings 5/12 (2.02 8-K) and was actually +4.75% over 5 days going into today, suggesting an initial positive reaction. Today's -5.17% on average volume with ITA -3.2% looks like sector-sympathy giving back the post-earnings pop. With volume only 1.04x, this is not a fundamental re-rating — but the give-back may have further to run if defense weakness persists, because the post-earnings bid was the only thing supporting it.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued defense sector weakness next week + DCO breaking below pre-earnings level on rising volume confirms further downside
discoveries.what_kills: If DCO holds the pre-earnings reference and defense ETF stabilizes, the give-back is complete and thesis breaks
discoveries.catalyst: Defense sector pullback after positive earnings reaction faded
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 8-K from 5/12 + +4.75% 5-day frames today's drop as sector-driven mean reversion of an earnings pop
research_pointers: Pull DCO earnings details to verify the initial bid was warranted
Track relative strength vs ITA next 3 sessions

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics
discoveries.setup: Unprofitable biotech in sympathy selloff with XBI -3.08% and rising long rates
discoveries.thesis: BEAM, CRSP, BBIO all down 4-5% with XBI -3% — small/mid biotech is duration-sensitive and 30Y at 5.1% (highest in a year) plus Fed hike repricing crushes discount-rate-sensitive names. No catalyst signals on BEAM. 5-day -13.6% indicates ongoing sector unwind. Magnitude is roughly 1.7x XBI which is normal beta for a clinical-stage gene editing name.
discoveries.what_confirms: If yields stay elevated and XBI continues lower, BEAM bleed continues at sector beta
discoveries.what_kills: If a positive clinical readout or M&A headline hits BEAM specifically, thesis breaks
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals dict + parallel CRSP/BBIO moves + XBI -3% confirms sector/rate driver
research_pointers: Watch yield curve — biotech beta to rates is asymmetric on the way down
Check upcoming BEAM clinical milestones that could break sector correlation

discoveries.name: WEX Inc.
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap fintech up 5.5% on a broadly red tape with only a routine 5.07 (annual meeting) 8-K
discoveries.thesis: WEX +5.5% with the tape down 1.2% and on AVERAGE volume (0.96x) is genuinely odd. The 8-K is shareholder vote results — that doesn't drive a 5% rally. Could be activist filing, takeout chatter (Magnum PE takeover news today might be reviving fintech buyout speculation), or a buyback authorization in the 8.01 exhibit. Without seeing the 8.01 content I can't articulate either direction at conf 3+: a buyback/strategic review would be UNDERDONE bullish, a vote-driven mechanical bid would be OVERDONE.
discoveries.catalyst: 8-K with annual meeting vote (5.07) + unidentified 8.01 exhibit; possible PE/activist chatter
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Average volume on a 5.5% green-against-red move with only routine filing items doesn't fit either a strong takeout bid or a clear fundamental catalyst
research_pointers: READ THE 8.01 EXHIBIT — most likely path to understanding this move
Check options flow for unusual call buying that would suggest takeover speculation
Scan for activist 13D filings on WEX

discoveries.name: Builders FirstSource
discoveries.setup: Housing-cycle name selling on Fed-hike repricing and 30Y at 5.1%
discoveries.thesis: BLDR is one of the most rate-sensitive industrials. Fed funds futures now pricing a HIKE by December plus 30Y at year-high yields directly translates to higher mortgage rates → fewer housing starts → less lumber/framing demand. -4.31% on below-avg volume is mechanical macro flow, magnitude reasonable. UFPI -4.19% confirms the housing-supplier sympathy. Not a mispricing — a textbook rate-sensitive reaction.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued upward pressure on 10Y/30Y yields extends the move; mortgage rate prints
discoveries.what_kills: Yield reversal or dovish Warsh commentary stabilizes the group
discoveries.catalyst: 30Y yields hit 5.1%, Fed hike now in futures curve
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No company filings + parallel UFPI move + clear macro rate catalyst in news flow
research_pointers: Watch for Warsh's first public commentary
discoveries.what_to_learn: Rate-sensitive industrials (builders, REITs, utilities) moving with yields on a Fed-repricing day are RATIONAL not mispricings — these are the cleanest macro transmissions and chasing them is a yield bet, not a stock bet.

catalyst_chains.primary_event: 30Y yield hits 5.1%, Fed funds futures price a hike — duration unwind
Defense sector weakness on Trump Taiwan ambiguity comments

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump on Taiwan: told Xi 'I don't talk about' whether U.S. would defend Taiwan from China
trump_signals.note: Strategic ambiguity comment plausibly behind defense ETF -3.2% today. Lower probability of Taiwan conflict reduces near-term defense procurement urgency premium. Watch whether this becomes a recurring theme or one-off comment.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump 1Q26 stock filings show purchases of AMZN, META, ORCL, AVGO, MOTOROLA, DELL plus Palantir trading
trump_signals.note: Disclosure is backward-looking (Q1 2026) and not actionable as a forward signal. Mention only because it could induce short-term retail follow-on flows in PLTR specifically given the Truth Social cross-promotion angle.

discovery.no_signals_note: Tonight's movers are dominated by macro transmission (yields, Fed hike repricing, defense rotation, biotech duration unwind) rather than idiosyncratic mispricings. The single genuinely odd mover is WEX +5.5% green-on-red with average volume and only a routine 8-K — that's the best research candidate for Michael, specifically reading the 8.01 exhibit.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts
ai_announcements.summary: OpenAI rolling out a personal finance dashboard within ChatGPT, allowing users to link bank accounts and view portfolio performance, spending, subscriptions, and upcoming payments.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: WEX is B2B fleet/corporate payments and healthcare benefits processing, not consumer PFM. ChatGPT consumer finance dashboard does not touch WEX's core. WEX move today (+5.52%) is more likely tied to its 5.07 8-K (annual meeting vote results) and unrelated to OpenAI news.
research_pointers: Check WEX 8-K from 5/14 - 5.07 item could include activist/board outcome
Confirm no overlap with consumer PFM (WEX is corporate cards/fleet/health)
Watch INTU, SOFI, NerdWallet (private) as more direct PFM competitors to ChatGPT finance

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most large stock moves in today's data appear unrelated to AI announcements. AEIS (-6%), KTOS (-5%), AVAV (-4%), DCO (-5%) are industrials/defense moves with their own catalysts (8-Ks, earnings, sector rotation post defense run). BEAM, BBIO, CRSP biotech weakness is sector-specific, not AI-driven. BLDR/UFPI weakness ties to housing/lumber, not AI. The OpenAI personal finance launch is the only clearly market-relevant AI item; the closest public comp would be INTU (not in move list). No Anthropic announcements in this batch.
